skill.name: delta-engage
skill.description: Finds high-fit posts to engage with across Reddit and LinkedIn based on the user's positioning and ICP, then drafts platform-appropriate engagement angles.
skill.summary: Social engagement assistant for Reddit and LinkedIn that finds relevant posts and drafts reply angles.
skill.author: newan2001

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 594 potential issues across 25 files, but all findings are false positives or expected behavior for a social media API tool. The 'weak crypto' and 'C2 keywords' alerts were triggered by documentation patterns and comment text (e.g., references to OAuth tokens, API keys, and Reddit URLs). The 'external_commands' hits in .md files are markdown backtick formatting, not shell execution. Network, filesystem, and env_access are required for the skill's core purpose of querying Reddit/LinkedIn/X APIs and managing user configuration files.

medium_findings.title: Network access to external APIs
medium_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP requests to Reddit, LinkedIn, and X APIs via Python requests library. This is required for the core functionality of fetching posts and engagement opportunities. All endpoints are documented social media APIs. Users must provide their own API tokens (APIFY_API_TOKEN, REDDIT_CLIENT_ID/SECRET) via environment variables.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct code review confirms standard API calls to documented social media endpoints. No data exfiltration patterns or suspicious domains detected.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem writes to user directories
medium_findings.description: Scripts write output JSON files and read configuration from user-specified paths. The setup script creates config directories under the user's home. This is expected for a CLI tool that persists state and user configuration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: File operations are limited to config and output directories specified by the user. No writes to system directories or sensitive paths observed.

low_findings.title: Environment variable access for API tokens
low_findings.description: Scripts read API tokens (APIFY_API_TOKEN, REDDIT_CLIENT_ID, REDDIT_CLIENT_SECRET) from environment variables. This is standard practice for credential management in CLI tools and avoids hardcoding secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard os.environ usage for user-provided API credentials. No environment variable exfiltration or system probing detected.

low_findings.title: Shell command usage in setup and rank scripts
low_findings.description: The setup script uses shell command substitution for environment detection, and rank.py has minimal shell interaction. These are standard CLI tool patterns.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Shell usage is for standard environment detection (checking for python3, pip). No arbitrary command execution from user input detected.

content.user_title: Find and engage with high-fit posts on Reddit and LinkedIn
content.value_statement: Manual social listening is time-consuming and inconsistent. This skill automates finding relevant posts, scoring them against your ICP, and drafting engagement angles so you can build presence faster.
actual_capabilities: Searches Reddit subreddits and LinkedIn for posts matching your positioning and ICP criteria
Scores and ranks posts by relevance, intent, and engagement potential
Drafts platform-appropriate comment angles tailored to each post
Supports configurable cadence (weekly, twice-weekly, custom) for engagement routines
Maintains a watchlist of accounts and topics to track for ongoing engagement opportunities
Generates a digest of selected posts with pre-drafted responses ready for review
limitations: Requires user-provided API tokens (Apify, Reddit OAuth) to function
Cannot post directly to platforms—all drafts require manual review and posting
Dependent on third-party API availability (Reddit, LinkedIn, X rate limits and changes)
English-language posts work best; non-English content may have reduced accuracy

use_cases.title: Weekly LinkedIn engagement routine
use_cases.description: A B2B founder wants to maintain visibility by commenting on 10 relevant LinkedIn posts each week. They configure their ICP and run the skill to get a curated list with draft angles.

use_cases.title: Reddit community building for SaaS
use_cases.description: A SaaS marketer wants to find Reddit threads where their target users discuss pain points. The skill identifies high-fit threads and drafts helpful, non-promotional responses.

use_cases.title: Personal brand growth for consultants
use_cases.description: An independent consultant wants to build authority on LinkedIn by engaging thoughtfully on industry posts. The skill surfaces trending discussions matching their expertise.

prompt_templates.title: Basic weekly engagement run
prompt_templates.prompt: Run delta-engage for this week. Find 10 posts across Reddit and LinkedIn that match my ICP, and draft engagement angles for each.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with a regular engagement routine

prompt_templates.title: Platform-specific search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Reddit only for posts about [topic] in the last 7 days. Focus on subreddits where my ICP is active. Draft comment angles that lead with a question.
prompt_templates.scenario: Targeting a specific platform and topic

prompt_templates.title: Watchlist review and engagement
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my watchlist accounts and topics. For any new posts from watched accounts or matching watched topics this week, draft engagement angles.
prompt_templates.scenario: Engaging with tracked accounts and topics

prompt_templates.title: Custom ICP and positioning setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up my delta-engage config. My positioning is [one-liner]. My ICP is [role, context, pain]. Exclude signals: [competitors, peers]. Run a dry-run digest.

output_examples.input: Run delta-engage for this week targeting SaaS founders struggling with customer churn.
output: Post: 'We lost 30% of our customers last quarter—what retention metrics actually matter?'
Angle (question-led): Ask about their current NPS cadence before suggesting tooling.
2. [LINKEDIN] post by @founderX · fit 8/10 · thought leader · 1d ago
Post: 'The biggest mistake I see in early-stage churn analysis...'
Angle (agree-and-extend): Validate their point and add a data point about cohort segmentation.

output_examples.input: Set up my delta-engage profile for LinkedIn-only engagement targeting HR leaders at mid-size companies.
output: ICP: HR leaders, mid-size companies, pain: employee retention

best_practices: Always review and personalize drafted comments before posting—authenticity matters more than volume
Start with a narrow ICP definition and expand once you see consistent quality matches
Use the question-led angle format for Reddit to invite discussion rather than promote
Set a realistic cadence (1-2x per week) to maintain quality over quantity
anti_patterns: Do not post drafted comments without review—context-specific edits improve authenticity
Do not configure an overly broad ICP hoping to maximize volume—quality matches are more valuable
Do not use the skill for direct product promotion—social engagement fails when it reads as marketing

faq.question: Which platforms does delta-engage support?
faq.answer: Currently supports Reddit, LinkedIn, and X (Twitter). You can enable any combination via the platforms config.

faq.question: Do I need API tokens to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You need an Apify API token for the default data source, or optional Reddit OAuth credentials. LinkedIn uses the same Apify token.

faq.question: Does the skill post automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill drafts engagement angles for your review. You must manually copy and post each comment to maintain authenticity.

faq.question: How does the skill score post relevance?
faq.answer: Posts are scored against your ICP definition (role, context, pain) and pain language keywords. Higher scores indicate stronger fit.

faq.question: Can I customize the engagement cadence?
faq.answer: Yes. The cadence config accepts weekly, mon_thu (twice weekly), or any custom display string. The skill tracks your routine.

faq.question: What languages are supported?
faq.answer: The skill works best with English content. Non-English posts may be matched but with reduced accuracy in scoring.
